entries.title: Incomplete cybersecurity risk assessment
entries.riskStatement: Incomplete cybersecurity risk assessment may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unknown risks remain untreated; management decisions are made with incomplete information.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis and information system security policies; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Enterprise services, critical processes, information systems, suppliers
entries.treatmentDirection: Establish a formal cyber risk assessment methodology and annual review cycle.
suggestedControls: Risk methodology, risk workshops, periodic risk assessment, risk scoring criteria

entries.title: Lack of documented information security policy
entries.riskStatement: Lack of documented information security policy may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Inconsistent security practices and weak enforceability of minimum requirements.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis and information system security policies; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Enterprise services, critical processes, information systems, suppliers
entries.treatmentDirection: Approve, communicate and periodically review information security policies.
suggestedControls: Information security policy framework, policy ownership, policy review workflow

entries.title: No defined risk appetite or tolerance
entries.riskStatement: No defined risk appetite or tolerance may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Poor risk decisions; unresolved disagreement on acceptable residual risk.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis and information system security policies; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Enterprise services, critical processes, information systems, suppliers
entries.treatmentDirection: Define management-approved cybersecurity risk appetite and tolerance thresholds.
suggestedControls: Risk appetite statement, risk matrix, escalation thresholds, acceptance criteria

entries.title: Critical assets are not identified
entries.riskStatement: Critical assets are not identified may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Key systems, applications, data and dependencies remain unprotected or under-prioritized.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) asset management; Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Assets, applications, cloud services, infrastructure, data repositories
entries.treatmentDirection: Maintain an asset and service inventory linked to business criticality.
suggestedControls: Asset inventory, classification, ownership, criticality assignment

entries.title: Critical business services are not mapped to supporting systems
entries.riskStatement: Critical business services are not mapped to supporting systems may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Inability to understand operational impact and prioritize resilience measures.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis; Art.21(2)(c) business continuity and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical services, process maps, ICT dependencies, suppliers
entries.treatmentDirection: Map critical services to systems, applications, suppliers, data and dependencies.
suggestedControls: Service dependency mapping, business impact analysis, criticality model

entries.title: Cyber risks are not assigned to owners
entries.riskStatement: Cyber risks are not assigned to owners may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: No accountability for treatment decisions, deadlines or residual-risk approval.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis and information system security policies; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Enterprise services, critical processes, information systems, suppliers
entries.treatmentDirection: Assign risk owners and action owners for every material cybersecurity risk.
suggestedControls: Risk ownership model, RACI matrix, risk assignment workflow

entries.title: Risk treatment actions are not tracked
entries.riskStatement: Risk treatment actions are not tracked may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Controls remain unimplemented and residual risk remains unknown.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis and information system security policies; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Enterprise services, critical processes, information systems, suppliers
entries.treatmentDirection: Maintain a risk treatment plan with owners, target dates, status and evidence.
suggestedControls: Risk treatment plan, remediation tracker, status reporting, overdue escalation

entries.title: Residual risks are accepted without approval
entries.riskStatement: Residual risks are accepted without approval may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Excessive or unmanaged exposure is retained without informed management accountability.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis and information system security policies; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Enterprise services, critical processes, information systems, suppliers
entries.treatmentDirection: Require formal residual-risk acceptance with justification and management approval.
suggestedControls: Residual risk approval workflow, acceptance thresholds, expiry dates

entries.title: Management does not oversee cybersecurity risk
entries.riskStatement: Management does not oversee cybersecurity risk may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Weak governance, poor prioritization and increased accountability exposure.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.20 management accountability; Art.21 cybersecurity risk-management measures
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Enterprise risk governance, cyber risk dashboard, management committees
entries.treatmentDirection: Establish recurring management oversight of cybersecurity risk and resilience.
suggestedControls: Management reporting, cyber risk committee, board updates, KPI/KRI monitoring

entries.title: Cybersecurity controls are not periodically reviewed
entries.riskStatement: Cybersecurity controls are not periodically reviewed may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Controls become outdated, ineffective or misaligned to current threats.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(f) policies and procedures to assess effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Controls, policies, technical safeguards, third-party controls
entries.treatmentDirection: Perform scheduled control effectiveness reviews and track improvement actions.
suggestedControls: Control testing, internal audits, maturity assessments, effectiveness KPIs

entries.title: Weak password practices
entries.riskStatement: Weak password practices may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Account compromise, credential stuffing and unauthorized access.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Users, privileged accounts, IAM, remote access, critical applications
entries.treatmentDirection: Enforce strong authentication and password policy controls.
suggestedControls: Password policy, banned passwords, account lockout, password manager guidance

entries.title: Absence of multi-factor authentication
entries.riskStatement: Absence of multi-factor authentication may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Higher likelihood of account takeover, especially for privileged and remote access.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Users, privileged accounts, IAM, remote access, critical applications
entries.treatmentDirection: Enable MFA for privileged accounts, remote access, cloud consoles and critical applications.
suggestedControls: MFA enforcement, conditional access, privileged MFA, exception reviews

entries.title: Excessive user privileges
entries.riskStatement: Excessive user privileges may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Privilege abuse, data exposure and lateral movement after compromise.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Users, privileged accounts, IAM, remote access, critical applications
entries.treatmentDirection: Apply least privilege and role-based access controls.
suggestedControls: RBAC, access request approvals, least privilege, privileged access reviews

entries.title: Privileged accounts are not monitored
entries.riskStatement: Privileged accounts are not monitored may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Misuse of admin access may remain undetected.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Privileged accounts, domain admins, cloud admins, database admins
entries.treatmentDirection: Implement privileged access management and monitor administrator activity.
suggestedControls: PAM, session logging, admin alerts, break-glass account controls

entries.title: Dormant or orphaned accounts remain active
entries.riskStatement: Dormant or orphaned accounts remain active may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unauthorized access after employee exit, role change or supplier termination.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: User accounts, supplier accounts, service accounts, SaaS accounts
entries.treatmentDirection: Automate and review joiner/mover/leaver account lifecycle processes.
suggestedControls: JML workflow, HR integration, periodic dormant account review

entries.title: Shared administrator accounts
entries.riskStatement: Shared administrator accounts may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Lack of accountability and weak traceability for privileged actions.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Administrative accounts, servers, databases, network devices
entries.treatmentDirection: Use named privileged accounts and eliminate shared administrator accounts where possible.
suggestedControls: Named admin accounts, PAM checkout, session recording, access attribution

entries.title: Poor access reviews
entries.riskStatement: Poor access reviews may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Accumulated access rights increase unauthorized access and fraud risk.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: IAM, applications, databases, file shares, SaaS platforms
entries.treatmentDirection: Perform periodic access recertification for users, privileged accounts and suppliers.
suggestedControls: Quarterly access reviews, privilege review, business owner certification

entries.title: Weak remote access controls
entries.riskStatement: Weak remote access controls may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unauthorized entry into internal systems or cloud environments.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: VPN, ZTNA, remote desktop, cloud admin portals
entries.treatmentDirection: Secure remote access using VPN/ZTNA, MFA, device checks and monitoring.
suggestedControls: ZTNA/VPN security, MFA, endpoint compliance checks, restricted RDP/SSH

entries.title: Lack of segregation of duties
entries.riskStatement: Lack of segregation of duties may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Fraud, unauthorized changes or unreviewed administrative activity.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Finance systems, admin roles, change management, production deployments
entries.treatmentDirection: Define incompatible roles and enforce segregation of duties for critical processes.
suggestedControls: SoD matrix, role design, conflict checks, compensating controls

entries.title: Inadequate HR security checks for sensitive roles
entries.riskStatement: Inadequate HR security checks for sensitive roles may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Insider risk and unauthorized access to critical information or systems.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) human resources security, access control and asset management; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/continuous authentication and secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Employees, contractors, privileged users, critical operations roles
entries.treatmentDirection: Apply appropriate screening, confidentiality obligations and role-based access controls.
suggestedControls: Pre-employment screening, confidentiality agreements, role-based onboarding

entries.title: Unsupported operating systems or software
entries.riskStatement: Unsupported operating systems or software may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Exploitation of known vulnerabilities and inability to receive security fixes.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Servers, endpoints, network devices, cloud infrastructure, security tools
entries.treatmentDirection: Upgrade, isolate or decommission unsupported systems.
suggestedControls: End-of-life inventory, upgrade roadmap, compensating controls, isolation

entries.title: Unpatched critical vulnerabilities
entries.riskStatement: Unpatched critical vulnerabilities may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: System compromise, ransomware, data breach or service disruption.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Servers, endpoints, network devices, cloud infrastructure, security tools
entries.treatmentDirection: Implement vulnerability scanning and SLA-based patch management.
suggestedControls: Vulnerability scanning, patch SLAs, emergency patch process, remediation tracking

entries.title: Poor network segmentation
entries.riskStatement: Poor network segmentation may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Rapid spread of attacks across systems and business services.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) secure maintenance; Art.21(2)(i) asset management; Art.21(2)(j) secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Networks, VLANs, firewalls, critical systems, OT/IT boundary
entries.treatmentDirection: Segment critical networks and restrict lateral movement.
suggestedControls: Network segmentation, firewall zones, microsegmentation, network access controls

entries.title: Internet-exposed services are not controlled
entries.riskStatement: Internet-exposed services are not controlled may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Increased attack surface and exploitation of exposed systems.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Public IPs, web apps, APIs, VPN, remote admin interfaces
entries.treatmentDirection: Maintain external attack-surface management and approval for exposed services.
suggestedControls: External asset inventory, exposure scans, change approvals, hardening

entries.title: Misconfigured firewalls or security groups
entries.riskStatement: Misconfigured firewalls or security groups may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unauthorized network access, data exposure or service compromise.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Firewalls, routers, cloud security groups, network ACLs
entries.treatmentDirection: Review and approve firewall and cloud security group rules.
suggestedControls: Firewall rule review, change control, least-access rules, rule recertification

entries.title: Insecure wireless networks
entries.riskStatement: Insecure wireless networks may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unauthorized access to internal resources or interception of traffic.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(j) secured communications; Art.21(2)(i) access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Wireless networks, guest Wi-Fi, employee endpoints
entries.treatmentDirection: Secure Wi-Fi using strong authentication, encryption and guest segregation.
suggestedControls: WPA3/enterprise authentication, guest isolation, wireless monitoring

entries.title: Lack of endpoint protection
entries.riskStatement: Lack of endpoint protection may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Malware infection, data theft and poor visibility into endpoint compromise.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Workstations, laptops, servers, mobile devices
entries.treatmentDirection: Deploy and monitor endpoint detection and response controls.
suggestedControls: EDR/anti-malware, tamper protection, detection policies, endpoint isolation

entries.title: Weak server hardening
entries.riskStatement: Weak server hardening may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Compromise of critical systems through unnecessary services or insecure configurations.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Servers, databases, network appliances, cloud workloads
entries.treatmentDirection: Apply secure configuration baselines and hardening standards.
suggestedControls: CIS baselines, configuration scanning, hardening checklists, exception tracking

entries.title: Insecure administrative interfaces
entries.riskStatement: Insecure administrative interfaces may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unauthorized system control through exposed management consoles.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Admin portals, SSH, RDP, hypervisors, cloud consoles, network management
entries.treatmentDirection: Restrict administrative interfaces and enforce MFA and network controls.
suggestedControls: Admin interface restrictions, bastion hosts, MFA, IP allowlisting, logging

entries.title: Lack of logging on critical systems
entries.riskStatement: Lack of logging on critical systems may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Incidents remain undetected or cannot be investigated.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Servers, applications, databases, network devices, cloud platforms
entries.treatmentDirection: Enable centralized logging for critical systems and security events.
suggestedControls: Centralized logging, log retention, log integrity, SIEM integration

entries.title: Security events are not monitored
entries.riskStatement: Security events are not monitored may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Delayed detection and containment of cyber incidents.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Security events, monitoring platforms, incident response process, critical services
entries.treatmentDirection: Implement SIEM/SOC or equivalent monitored logging and alerting.
suggestedControls: SIEM/SOC monitoring, alert rules, escalation playbooks, log source coverage

entries.title: No incident response plan
entries.riskStatement: No incident response plan may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Chaotic or delayed response during cyber incidents.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Incident response team, critical systems, communication channels
entries.treatmentDirection: Create, approve and test a cybersecurity incident response plan.
suggestedControls: IR plan, response playbooks, severity model, crisis communications

entries.title: Incident roles and responsibilities are unclear
entries.riskStatement: Incident roles and responsibilities are unclear may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Delayed containment, investigation and recovery.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Incident response team, management, legal, communications, IT operations
entries.treatmentDirection: Define incident response roles, escalation paths and responsibilities.
suggestedControls: IR RACI, escalation matrix, on-call rota, management notification process

entries.title: Incidents are not classified by severity
entries.riskStatement: Incidents are not classified by severity may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Incorrect response prioritization and regulatory reporting decisions.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; NIS2 incident notification obligations
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Incident records, service impact, user impact, regulatory reporting workflow
entries.treatmentDirection: Define incident classification criteria including impact, severity and reporting triggers.
suggestedControls: Severity matrix, incident classification workflow, reporting trigger checklist

entries.title: NIS2-reportable incidents are not escalated on time
entries.riskStatement: NIS2-reportable incidents are not escalated on time may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Regulatory breach, sanctions and loss of supervisory confidence.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.23 incident reporting obligations; Art.21(2)(b) incident handling
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Incident process, legal/compliance, competent authority communication
entries.treatmentDirection: Implement regulatory incident-reporting workflow and escalation deadlines.
suggestedControls: Regulatory reporting procedure, notification timeline tracker, legal review workflow

entries.title: No forensic readiness
entries.riskStatement: No forensic readiness may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Loss of evidence and inability to investigate incidents properly.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Logs, endpoints, servers, cloud systems, forensic evidence
entries.treatmentDirection: Preserve relevant logs, evidence and chain of custody.
suggestedControls: Forensic readiness plan, evidence retention, chain-of-custody process

entries.title: No lessons-learned process after incidents
entries.riskStatement: No lessons-learned process after incidents may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Repeated incidents and failure to improve controls.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Incident process, controls, remediation actions
entries.treatmentDirection: Conduct post-incident reviews and track corrective actions.
suggestedControls: Post-incident review, root-cause analysis, corrective action tracking

entries.title: Alert fatigue or unmanaged alerts
entries.riskStatement: Alert fatigue or unmanaged alerts may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Critical attacks are missed due to excessive false positives or weak triage.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: SIEM, EDR, network alerts, cloud security alerts
entries.treatmentDirection: Define alert triage, tuning and escalation processes.
suggestedControls: Alert tuning, severity classification, triage queues, SOC metrics

entries.title: Lack of threat intelligence use
entries.riskStatement: Lack of threat intelligence use may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Poor awareness of active threats and delayed defensive actions.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis; Art.21(2)(b) incident handling
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Threat intelligence, SIEM, vulnerability management, risk register
entries.treatmentDirection: Integrate threat intelligence into monitoring, vulnerability prioritization and risk assessment.
suggestedControls: Threat intel feeds, IOC enrichment, threat briefings, risk updates

entries.title: DDoS attacks disrupt public services
entries.riskStatement: DDoS attacks disrupt public services may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Service unavailability, reputational damage and operational disruption.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity; Art.21(2)(b) incident handling
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Public websites, APIs, DNS, internet connectivity, customer portals
entries.treatmentDirection: Use DDoS protection and resilience planning for internet-facing services.
suggestedControls: DDoS protection, CDN/WAF, runbooks, ISP escalation, traffic monitoring

entries.title: Sensitive data is not classified
entries.riskStatement: Sensitive data is not classified may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Inadequate protection of confidential, personal or critical operational data.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis; Art.21(2)(h) cryptography; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Sensitive data, databases, file shares, backups, logs, communication channels
entries.treatmentDirection: Establish and apply a data classification scheme.
suggestedControls: Data classification policy, data inventory, labeling, handling rules

entries.title: Sensitive data is stored without encryption
entries.riskStatement: Sensitive data is stored without encryption may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Data exposure after system compromise, theft or unauthorized access.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(h) policies and procedures regarding cryptography and encryption; Art.21(2)(j) secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Databases, storage, endpoints, backups, cloud storage
entries.treatmentDirection: Encrypt sensitive data at rest using approved cryptographic standards.
suggestedControls: Encryption at rest, key management, database encryption, disk encryption

entries.title: Data is transmitted without encryption
entries.riskStatement: Data is transmitted without encryption may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Interception, manipulation or disclosure of data in transit.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(h) policies and procedures regarding cryptography and encryption; Art.21(2)(j) secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Networks, APIs, email, file transfer, web applications
entries.treatmentDirection: Enforce secure communications for internal and external data transfers.
suggestedControls: TLS, secure file transfer, VPN/ZTNA, certificate management

entries.title: Poor encryption-key management
entries.riskStatement: Poor encryption-key management may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Loss of confidentiality or availability due to exposed, weak or lost keys.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(h) policies and procedures regarding cryptography and encryption; Art.21(2)(j) secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Encryption keys, HSM/KMS, certificates, secrets
entries.treatmentDirection: Implement formal cryptographic key lifecycle management.
suggestedControls: Key management policy, KMS/HSM, rotation, access controls, backup keys

entries.title: Excessive data retention
entries.riskStatement: Excessive data retention may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Increased breach impact and privacy/compliance exposure.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(a) risk analysis; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Data repositories, archives, backups, logs, SaaS platforms
entries.treatmentDirection: Define and enforce data retention and secure deletion rules.
suggestedControls: Data retention schedule, deletion workflow, archive controls

entries.title: Data leakage through email or cloud sharing
entries.riskStatement: Data leakage through email or cloud sharing may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Confidentiality breach, regulatory exposure and reputational damage.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(h) encryption; Art.21(2)(j) secured communications; Art.21(2)(i) access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Email, collaboration platforms, cloud storage, file sharing
entries.treatmentDirection: Deploy data leakage prevention and secure sharing controls.
suggestedControls: DLP, sharing restrictions, external collaboration controls, encryption

entries.title: Backups contain unprotected sensitive data
entries.riskStatement: Backups contain unprotected sensitive data may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Sensitive data exposure through backup compromise or theft.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) backup management; Art.21(2)(h) encryption
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Backup systems, backup media, recovery vaults, cloud backup
entries.treatmentDirection: Encrypt backups and restrict access to backup repositories.
suggestedControls: Backup encryption, access restrictions, immutable backups, backup monitoring

entries.title: Poor database access controls
entries.riskStatement: Poor database access controls may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unauthorized access to critical business or personal data.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) access control; Art.21(2)(h) encryption
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Databases, data warehouses, reporting platforms
entries.treatmentDirection: Restrict and monitor database access using least privilege and logging.
suggestedControls: Database access review, privileged database monitoring, encryption, activity logging

entries.title: Inadequate protection of logs
entries.riskStatement: Inadequate protection of logs may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Log tampering, loss of evidence or exposure of sensitive information.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment; Art.21(2)(h) encryption
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Security logs, system logs, audit logs, SIEM
entries.treatmentDirection: Protect log integrity, confidentiality and retention.
suggestedControls: Log integrity protection, restricted access, encryption, retention policy

entries.title: Lack of secure disposal procedures
entries.riskStatement: Lack of secure disposal procedures may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Data leakage from retired equipment, media or cloud resources.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) asset management; Art.21(2)(h) encryption
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Laptops, servers, storage media, mobile devices, cloud volumes
entries.treatmentDirection: Apply secure wiping and disposal procedures for ICT assets and media.
suggestedControls: Secure disposal policy, media sanitization, disposal certificates, asset offboarding

entries.title: No business continuity plan for cyber scenarios
entries.riskStatement: No business continuity plan for cyber scenarios may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Prolonged service outage and uncoordinated business response after a cyber incident.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical services, recovery systems, backups, crisis management, continuity plans
entries.treatmentDirection: Create and test a cyber-focused business continuity plan.
suggestedControls: Cyber BCP, continuity scenarios, call trees, service continuity procedures

entries.title: No disaster recovery plan
entries.riskStatement: No disaster recovery plan may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Inability to restore critical systems within business tolerance.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical systems, recovery infrastructure, applications, data
entries.treatmentDirection: Define disaster recovery strategy and recovery priorities.
suggestedControls: DR plan, recovery tiers, DR architecture, recovery runbooks

entries.title: Backups are not tested
entries.riskStatement: Backups are not tested may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Failed recovery during ransomware or destructive incident.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Backups, recovery vaults, critical applications, databases
entries.treatmentDirection: Test backup restoration regularly and track failed restore actions.
suggestedControls: Restore testing, backup verification, restore SLAs, test evidence

entries.title: Backups are connected to production systems
entries.riskStatement: Backups are connected to production systems may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Backups may be encrypted, deleted or corrupted by attackers.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Backup repositories, storage, backup admin accounts, recovery vaults
entries.treatmentDirection: Use immutable, offline or logically isolated backups.
suggestedControls: Immutable backups, offline copies, backup network isolation, admin segregation

entries.title: Recovery time objectives are not defined
entries.riskStatement: Recovery time objectives are not defined may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Business disruption exceeds management tolerance.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical services, applications, databases, service owners
entries.treatmentDirection: Define RTO/RPO for critical services and align backup/recovery capabilities.
suggestedControls: BIA, RTO/RPO definition, service criticality tiers, recovery planning

entries.title: Crisis management is not tested
entries.riskStatement: Crisis management is not tested may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Poor executive coordination and communication during major cyber incidents.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Management body, crisis team, communications, legal/compliance
entries.treatmentDirection: Conduct crisis exercises and executive tabletop testing.
suggestedControls: Crisis management exercises, tabletop scenarios, communication templates

entries.title: Single points of failure in critical systems
entries.riskStatement: Single points of failure in critical systems may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Service interruption if one component, provider or location fails.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical infrastructure, network paths, systems, suppliers, locations
entries.treatmentDirection: Identify and reduce single points of failure in critical services.
suggestedControls: Architecture review, redundancy, failover, dependency analysis

entries.title: Power, telecom or data-centre dependency failure
entries.riskStatement: Power, telecom or data-centre dependency failure may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Loss of service availability due to infrastructure or provider outage.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Data centres, telecom lines, power, cloud regions, facilities
entries.treatmentDirection: Build redundancy and supplier continuity plans for critical infrastructure dependencies.
suggestedControls: Redundant power/connectivity, provider SLAs, continuity testing, alternate sites

entries.title: No manual workaround for critical processes
entries.riskStatement: No manual workaround for critical processes may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Operations stop completely during ICT outage.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical business processes, service desks, customer support, operations
entries.treatmentDirection: Define manual fallback procedures for critical business processes.
suggestedControls: Manual workaround procedures, process owner training, fallback forms

entries.title: Recovery priorities are not aligned with business impact
entries.riskStatement: Recovery priorities are not aligned with business impact may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Wrong systems are restored first and critical services remain unavailable.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity, backup management, disaster recovery and crisis management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical services, applications, recovery plans, DR runbooks
entries.treatmentDirection: Align recovery sequence with business impact analysis and criticality.
suggestedControls: BIA-based recovery prioritization, recovery runbooks, management approval

entries.title: Suppliers are not risk assessed
entries.riskStatement: Suppliers are not risk assessed may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Hidden third-party exposure affecting critical services and data.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Suppliers, ICT providers, cloud services, outsourced services, subcontractors
entries.treatmentDirection: Perform supplier cybersecurity assessment before onboarding and periodically thereafter.
suggestedControls: Supplier risk assessment, due diligence, criticality classification, reassessment cycle

entries.title: Supplier contracts lack security requirements
entries.riskStatement: Supplier contracts lack security requirements may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Weak contractual protection and unclear responsibilities during incidents.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Contracts, SLAs, outsourced services, cloud providers
entries.treatmentDirection: Add cybersecurity, resilience and reporting clauses to supplier contracts.
suggestedControls: Security clauses, SLAs, audit rights, incident notification, data protection terms

entries.title: Critical suppliers lack incident notification obligations
entries.riskStatement: Critical suppliers lack incident notification obligations may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Late awareness of supplier incidents and delayed regulatory response.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical suppliers, cloud providers, managed service providers
entries.treatmentDirection: Require timely supplier incident notification and escalation procedures.
suggestedControls: Incident notification clauses, supplier escalation contacts, reporting SLAs

entries.title: Cloud provider misconfiguration
entries.riskStatement: Cloud provider misconfiguration may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Data exposure, service compromise or unauthorized access due to shared responsibility gaps.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Cloud tenants, storage buckets, IAM, cloud networks, SaaS platforms
entries.treatmentDirection: Review cloud configuration and shared responsibility controls.
suggestedControls: Cloud security posture management, configuration baselines, shared responsibility matrix

entries.title: Supplier remote access is uncontrolled
entries.riskStatement: Supplier remote access is uncontrolled may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unauthorized access through vendor accounts or remote maintenance tools.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Vendor accounts, remote access tools, VPN/ZTNA, privileged access
entries.treatmentDirection: Restrict, approve and monitor supplier remote access.
suggestedControls: Supplier access approval, MFA, time-bound access, session monitoring, PAM

entries.title: Concentration risk on one ICT provider
entries.riskStatement: Concentration risk on one ICT provider may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Major outage or operational disruption if a single supplier fails.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(c) business continuity
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Cloud providers, managed service providers, telecom providers, outsourced platforms
entries.treatmentDirection: Assess concentration risk and define resilience or exit options.
suggestedControls: Provider concentration analysis, contingency planning, multi-provider strategy, exit plan
entries.defaultOwnerRole: Third-Party Risk Manager / Business Continuity Manager

entries.title: Supplier subcontractors are unknown
entries.riskStatement: Supplier subcontractors are unknown may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Uncontrolled fourth-party exposure and lack of visibility into supply chain dependencies.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Fourth parties, subcontractors, outsourced ICT chains
entries.treatmentDirection: Require subcontractor transparency and change notification for critical suppliers.
suggestedControls: Subcontractor register, supplier disclosure clauses, approval/notification process

entries.title: No right to audit critical suppliers
entries.riskStatement: No right to audit critical suppliers may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Inability to verify supplier controls or investigate resilience concerns.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition, development and maintenance
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical supplier contracts, audit reports, assurance documentation
entries.treatmentDirection: Include audit, assurance and evidence rights for critical suppliers.
suggestedControls: Right-to-audit clauses, assurance reports, supplier review meetings

entries.title: No supplier exit plan
entries.riskStatement: No supplier exit plan may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Dependency lock-in and inability to transition services during supplier failure.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(c) business continuity
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Critical suppliers, data, service transition plans, alternative providers
entries.treatmentDirection: Maintain exit and transition plans for critical ICT suppliers.
suggestedControls: Exit plan, transition scenarios, data portability, termination support clauses

entries.title: Software supply-chain compromise
entries.riskStatement: Software supply-chain compromise may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Malware, backdoor or vulnerable component introduced into systems or products.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(e) secure development and vulnerability handling
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Software vendors, packages, CI/CD, source code, dependencies
entries.treatmentDirection: Use secure software supply-chain controls and verify components.
suggestedControls: SBOM, code signing, dependency scanning, vendor assurance, secure CI/CD

entries.title: Applications are developed without security requirements
entries.riskStatement: Applications are developed without security requirements may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Vulnerable systems and missed security controls during design.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Applications, APIs, source code, CI/CD, software components, development environments
entries.treatmentDirection: Integrate cybersecurity requirements into the software development lifecycle.
suggestedControls: Secure SDLC, security requirements, threat modeling, design review

entries.title: Code is deployed without security testing
entries.riskStatement: Code is deployed without security testing may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Exploitable application flaws are released to production.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Source code, web applications, APIs, production deployments
entries.treatmentDirection: Use SAST, DAST, code review and penetration testing before release.
suggestedControls: SAST/DAST, manual code review, release gates, penetration testing

entries.title: Vulnerable open-source components
entries.riskStatement: Vulnerable open-source components may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Compromise through known CVEs in third-party libraries.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Open-source libraries, packages, containers, software dependencies
entries.treatmentDirection: Use software composition analysis and dependency update processes.
suggestedControls: SCA, dependency management, CVE triage, SBOM

entries.title: No vulnerability disclosure process
entries.riskStatement: No vulnerability disclosure process may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Externally discovered vulnerabilities remain unmanaged or mishandled.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: External researchers, product teams, security mailbox, vulnerability tickets
entries.treatmentDirection: Establish a vulnerability disclosure and coordinated response process.
suggestedControls: Vulnerability disclosure policy, security contact, triage workflow, remediation SLA

entries.title: Weak API authentication
entries.riskStatement: Weak API authentication may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unauthorized access to data or services through exposed APIs.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) secure development; Art.21(2)(j) MFA/secured communications; Art.21(2)(i) access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: APIs, integration services, customer portals, mobile backends
entries.treatmentDirection: Secure APIs with strong authentication, authorization, throttling and logging.
suggestedControls: API authentication, authorization checks, rate limiting, API gateway, logging

entries.title: Poor change management
entries.riskStatement: Poor change management may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Outages, insecure changes or unauthorized modifications to production systems.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) secure maintenance; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Production systems, applications, network devices, cloud infrastructure
entries.treatmentDirection: Formalize change approval, testing and rollback procedures.
suggestedControls: Change management workflow, risk-based approvals, rollback plans, emergency change review

entries.title: Secrets stored in code repositories
entries.riskStatement: Secrets stored in code repositories may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Credential theft and unauthorized access to systems, APIs or cloud services.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Source repositories, CI/CD, API keys, cloud credentials, service accounts
entries.treatmentDirection: Use secrets management and prevent secrets from being committed to repositories.
suggestedControls: Secrets scanning, secrets vault, token rotation, repository protections

entries.title: Insecure CI/CD pipeline
entries.riskStatement: Insecure CI/CD pipeline may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Malicious or vulnerable code may be built and deployed to production.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: CI/CD tools, build agents, deployment credentials, code repositories
entries.treatmentDirection: Secure build and deployment pipelines with least privilege, signing and approvals.
suggestedControls: CI/CD hardening, branch protection, artifact signing, build isolation, approvals

entries.title: Lack of environment segregation
entries.riskStatement: Lack of environment segregation may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Testing or development activity impacts production or exposes production data.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Development environments, test systems, production systems, data sets
entries.treatmentDirection: Separate development, test and production environments with distinct access controls.
suggestedControls: Environment segregation, production data masking, access separation, deployment controls

entries.title: Uncontrolled software acquisition
entries.riskStatement: Uncontrolled software acquisition may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Shadow IT, unsupported tools and unassessed security exposure.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) secure acquisition; Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: SaaS, desktop software, business applications, open-source tools
entries.treatmentDirection: Approve, inventory and risk-assess software before acquisition and use.
suggestedControls: Software approval process, SaaS inventory, security assessment, procurement gates

entries.title: Phishing attacks compromise users
entries.riskStatement: Phishing attacks compromise users may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Credential theft, malware infection or unauthorized access to business systems.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(g) basic cyber hygiene and cybersecurity training; Art.21(2)(i) HR security and access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Employees, contractors, helpdesk, privileged users, endpoints, collaboration tools
entries.treatmentDirection: Run awareness training, phishing simulations and email security controls.
suggestedControls: Email security, phishing simulations, user reporting button, awareness training

entries.title: Social engineering against helpdesk
entries.riskStatement: Social engineering against helpdesk may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Account takeover through fraudulent reset or support requests.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(g) basic cyber hygiene and cybersecurity training; Art.21(2)(i) HR security and access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Helpdesk, IAM, password reset, privileged users
entries.treatmentDirection: Strengthen helpdesk identity verification and high-risk request approvals.
suggestedControls: Identity verification scripts, callback procedures, MFA reset approvals, helpdesk training

entries.title: Employees lack cyber hygiene training
entries.riskStatement: Employees lack cyber hygiene training may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Increased human-error incidents and policy non-compliance.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(g) basic cyber hygiene and cybersecurity training; Art.21(2)(i) HR security and access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Employees, contractors, privileged users, business process owners
entries.treatmentDirection: Conduct periodic cybersecurity training and role-specific education.
suggestedControls: Annual training, role-specific training, training completion tracking, policy awareness

entries.title: Malicious insider activity
entries.riskStatement: Malicious insider activity may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Data theft, sabotage, fraud or unauthorized disclosure.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(g) basic cyber hygiene and cybersecurity training; Art.21(2)(i) HR security and access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Employees, contractors, privileged accounts, sensitive data
entries.treatmentDirection: Monitor privileged activity, enforce segregation of duties and apply HR security controls.
suggestedControls: Insider risk monitoring, SoD, privileged monitoring, exit controls

entries.title: Accidental data disclosure
entries.riskStatement: Accidental data disclosure may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Confidentiality breach caused by user error or misdirected communication.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(g) basic cyber hygiene and cybersecurity training; Art.21(2)(i) HR security and access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Email, cloud sharing, documents, customer data
entries.treatmentDirection: Train users and apply DLP and secure sharing controls.
suggestedControls: DLP, user training, secure sharing rules, classification labels

entries.title: Uncontrolled use of removable media
entries.riskStatement: Uncontrolled use of removable media may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Malware introduction or data leakage through USB/removable devices.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(g) cyber hygiene; Art.21(2)(i) asset management and access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Endpoints, removable media, sensitive data, OT systems
entries.treatmentDirection: Restrict and monitor removable media use.
suggestedControls: USB control, media encryption, exception approvals, endpoint policy

entries.title: Use of personal devices without controls
entries.riskStatement: Use of personal devices without controls may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Data exposure, malware infection or unmanaged access to business systems.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(g) cyber hygiene; Art.21(2)(i) access control; Art.21(2)(j) secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Mobile devices, laptops, cloud apps, remote access
entries.treatmentDirection: Implement BYOD/mobile device policy and device compliance controls.
suggestedControls: MDM, conditional access, BYOD policy, device encryption

entries.title: Poor security culture
entries.riskStatement: Poor security culture may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Persistent policy non-compliance and low reporting of suspicious activity.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(g) basic cyber hygiene and cybersecurity training; Art.21(2)(i) HR security and access control
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Employees, management, business units, security awareness programme
entries.treatmentDirection: Run awareness campaigns and reinforce security expectations through management.
suggestedControls: Security culture campaigns, leadership communications, reporting channels, KPIs

entries.title: Lack of disciplinary process for security violations
entries.riskStatement: Lack of disciplinary process for security violations may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Weak enforcement of cybersecurity policies and repeated non-compliance.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(g) cyber hygiene; Art.21(2)(i) HR security
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Employees, contractors, policy violations, HR procedures
entries.treatmentDirection: Define disciplinary and corrective action process for policy violations.
suggestedControls: Policy violation procedure, HR escalation, corrective actions, repeat violation monitoring

entries.title: Key cybersecurity knowledge depends on one person
entries.riskStatement: Key cybersecurity knowledge depends on one person may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Operational dependency and poor resilience if key staff are unavailable.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(c) business continuity; Art.21(2)(g) training
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Security operations, IT operations, admin procedures, critical systems
entries.treatmentDirection: Document procedures and cross-train staff for critical cybersecurity operations.
suggestedControls: Procedure documentation, cross-training, succession planning, runbooks

entries.title: Unauthorized physical access to ICT areas
entries.riskStatement: Unauthorized physical access to ICT areas may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Tampering, theft or disruption of critical ICT infrastructure.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21 all-hazards approach; Art.21(2)(c) business continuity; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Server rooms, data centres, network cabinets, backup media
entries.treatmentDirection: Control and monitor physical access to server rooms and network areas.
suggestedControls: Physical access control, visitor logs, restricted areas, CCTV, access reviews

entries.title: Fire, flood or environmental event affects ICT systems
entries.riskStatement: Fire, flood or environmental event affects ICT systems may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Service outage, equipment damage or data loss.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21 all-hazards approach; Art.21(2)(c) business continuity; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Server rooms, data centres, network rooms, backup systems
entries.treatmentDirection: Implement environmental protections and continuity measures.
suggestedControls: Fire suppression, water detection, UPS, environmental monitoring, DR plan

entries.title: CCTV/access logs are not reviewed
entries.riskStatement: CCTV/access logs are not reviewed may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Physical incidents or unauthorized access may go undetected.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21 all-hazards approach; Art.21(2)(c) business continuity; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Physical access systems, CCTV, visitor logs, server rooms
entries.treatmentDirection: Monitor, retain and periodically review physical security logs.
suggestedControls: Access log review, CCTV retention, exception alerts, periodic audits

entries.title: OT/ICS systems are connected without segmentation
entries.riskStatement: OT/ICS systems are connected without segmentation may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Operational disruption, safety impact or lateral movement from IT to OT.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21 all-hazards approach; Art.21(2)(e) secure maintenance; Art.21(2)(i) asset management
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: OT/ICS networks, production systems, industrial controllers, IT/OT gateways
entries.treatmentDirection: Segment OT from IT networks and strictly control remote access.
suggestedControls: IT/OT segmentation, firewall rules, jump servers, OT access monitoring

entries.title: Legacy OT systems cannot be patched
entries.riskStatement: Legacy OT systems cannot be patched may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Persistent exploitation risk due to unpatched operational systems.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(e) vulnerability handling; Art.21 all-hazards approach
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Legacy OT systems, industrial controllers, engineering workstations
entries.treatmentDirection: Apply compensating controls, isolation and OT monitoring.
suggestedControls: Compensating controls, vulnerability risk assessment, network isolation, vendor support plan

entries.title: Remote maintenance of OT is insecure
entries.riskStatement: Remote maintenance of OT is insecure may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Unauthorized operational access or compromise via vendor maintenance channels.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(d) supply-chain security; Art.21(2)(i) access control; Art.21(2)(j) secured communications
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: OT remote access, vendor accounts, maintenance tools, jump servers
entries.treatmentDirection: Secure vendor remote maintenance with MFA, approvals, monitoring and time-bound access.
suggestedControls: Vendor access approval, MFA, session recording, time-bound access, jump host
entries.defaultOwnerRole: OT Security Owner / Third-Party Risk Manager

entries.title: Lack of OT incident response procedures
entries.riskStatement: Lack of OT incident response procedures may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Delayed containment and unsafe response in industrial or operational environments.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21 all-hazards approach
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: OT/ICS environments, operations teams, safety systems, incident response team
entries.treatmentDirection: Create OT-specific incident response playbooks and escalation procedures.
suggestedControls: OT incident playbooks, IT/OT escalation, safe shutdown procedures, exercises

entries.title: Inadequate monitoring of critical infrastructure systems
entries.riskStatement: Inadequate monitoring of critical infrastructure systems may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Attacks or anomalies in critical systems remain undetected.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(b) incident handling; Art.21(2)(f) effectiveness assessment
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: OT networks, critical infrastructure systems, SCADA, industrial sensors
entries.treatmentDirection: Deploy OT/critical-infrastructure-aware monitoring and alerting.
suggestedControls: OT monitoring, anomaly detection, asset visibility, alert escalation

entries.title: Insecure IoT devices
entries.riskStatement: Insecure IoT devices may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Botnet participation, lateral movement, surveillance or data leakage.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(i) asset management; Art.21(2)(e) secure maintenance
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: IoT devices, cameras, sensors, smart devices, building systems
entries.treatmentDirection: Inventory and harden IoT devices and restrict their network access.
suggestedControls: IoT inventory, default credential removal, segmentation, firmware updates

entries.title: Telecom or network equipment compromise
entries.riskStatement: Telecom or network equipment compromise may affect the confidentiality, integrity, availability or resilience of critical services and network and information systems.
entries.typicalImpact: Service disruption, interception or loss of network integrity.
entries.regulatoryMapping: Art.21(2)(j) secured communications; Art.21(2)(e) secure maintenance
entries.affectedAssetsOrServices: Routers, switches, firewalls, telecom links, DNS, network management
entries.treatmentDirection: Secure and monitor network and telecom infrastructure.
suggestedControls: Network device hardening, firmware updates, configuration backup, monitoring, access control
